perturbation.original_text: Following the acquisition of CIHRUS by QIWI and in order to promote their mutual interests, the parties wish to cooperate for their mutual benefit in the fields of their business, including digital payments, and establish a steering committee in order to analyse and develop potential areas of cooperation between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the acquisition of CIHRUS by QIWI and in order to promote their mutual interests, the parties wish to collaborate solely on banking projects that benefit both parties. All other forms of collaboration are explicitly excluded. To facilitate the collaboration, the parties will establish a steering committee in order to analyse and develop potential areas of cooperation between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text suggests a broad cooperation in the fields of their business, including digital payments. However, the modified version restricts this collaboration solely to banking projects. This change creates an in-text contradiction because it limits the scope of cooperation to only banking projects, whereas later clauses continue to discuss cooperation in broader terms (e.g., Clause 3.6 which includes items beyond purely banking projects such as 'Separation of NKO Rapida's digital wallet business'). The contradiction lies between the limited scope introduced here and the more general discussions of potential cooperation areas in later clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that the following items are potential areas of joint cooperation to be discussed, among other matters, at Steering Committee meetings: (a) Participation of Otkritie as a privileged party in banking projects established by QIWI where QIWI seeks a banking partner; (b) Subject to Applicable Law, commercial terms for QIWI to provide consumer scoring data it has collected to Otkritie; (c) Commercial terms for Otkritie to act as settlement bank of NKO Rapida in accordance with Clause 4.1; (d) Commercial terms relating to the amounts of deposits to be placed by NKO Rapida in Otkritie; (e) Separation of NKO Rapida's digital wallet business from the core business of NKO Rapida; (f) Commercial terms of cooperation of the parties in the areas of pre-paid cards and loyalty programs; (g) Commercial terms of the use by Otkritie of the NKO Rapida brands owned by QIWI Group; and (h) Commercial terms of the potential sale of the NKO Rapida's banking license to Otkritie.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the cooperation solely to banking projects, explicitly excluding all other forms of collaboration. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which lists potential areas of joint cooperation including items beyond banking projects such as 'Separation of NKO Rapida's digital wallet business' and 'Commercial terms of cooperation of the parties in the areas of pre-paid cards and loyalty programs', creating a clear inconsistency regarding the scope of collaboration. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Steering Committee shall meet at least once in each month and as otherwise agreed between Committee Member. Such meetings shall take place in Moscow, in a location agreed by Otkritie and QIWI Group or by telephone.
perturbation.changed_text: The Cooperation Committee shall meet at least once per quarter, unless all members agree otherwise. All meetings must be in person, in Moscow, at a location agreed by Otkritie and QIWI Group; telephone meetings are strictly prohibited.
perturbation.explanation: Here, "Steering Committee" is changed to "Cooperation Committee", and the meeting frequency is reduced from monthly to quarterly. The original text allowed for meetings to occur via telephone but that is also prohibited in the modified text. This contradicts the initial definition in Clause 3.1 which defines the committee as 'Steering Committee' and the term is used throughout the agreement. This difference in name and procedures creates uncertainty and makes Clause 3.1 contradictory in terms of the name and other clauses using the defined name, and uncertainty regarding meeting requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall form a steering committee (the "Steering Committee"), which shall analyse and develop potential areas of strategic cooperation between the Parties and oversee the Parties' cooperation in these areas.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Steering Committee" with "Cooperation Committee" and alters the meeting frequency and method. Clause 3.1, the contradicted text, defines the committee as "Steering Committee". This change directly contradicts the defined terminology, creating uncertainty and potentially rendering clauses that refer to the 'Steering Committee' unenforceable. The change also dictates a different frequency of meetings and bans telephone meetings, which were originally permitted, further solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For two (2) years following the Effective Time, QIWI covenants that at least one half of the total transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such two-year period shall be settled through PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie, provided that QIWI may terminate its obligation under this Clause 4.1 at any time, in which case it shall pay to Otkritie a fee equal to the demonstrated costs incurred by Otkritie for such period starting from 1 September 2014, until the date of termination of QIWI's obligations under this Clause 4.1 in connection with (i) the transfer of settlement bank function to PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie ; and (ii) supporting the settlement bank functionality for the Contact money transfer system, provided further that such fee shall in no event exceed RUB 50 000 000 (fifty million roubles).
perturbation.changed_text: For three (3) years following the Effective Time, QIWI ensures that all transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such three-year period shall be settled exclusively through Otkritie Bank, without any exceptions. QIWI may not terminate this obligation under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The original Clause 4.1 allows QIWI to settle at least one-half of transactions through Otkritie and provides an option for QIWI to terminate the obligation by paying a fee. This contrasts with the modified version, which requires QIWI to settle *all* transactions *exclusively* through Otkritie Bank for three years, and *removes* the termination clause entirely. This creates a direct contradiction regarding QIWI's obligations and termination rights. This changes 'PJSC Khanty-Mansiysk Bank Otkritie' to 'Otkritie Bank'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For three (3) years following the Effective Time, QIWI ensures that all transactions processed by the Contact money transfer system during such three-year period shall be settled exclusively through Otkritie Bank, without any exceptions. QIWI may not terminate this obligation under any circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and contradicted text are identical. While the perturbation explanation details how the 'Changed Text' contradicts the 'Original Text', the 'Changed Text' and 'Contradicted Text' do not present an in-text contradiction, as they are the same. They simply represent the modified obligation.
